summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia region and a MiG-31K carrying an X-47M2 Kinzhal missile prompted nationwide air alerts. Ukraine reported multiple UAV incursions across northern and southern oblasts, including a Romanian drone strike on a Galati building. Russia prepared a massed strike with six Tu‑95MS, two Tu‑160 bombers and up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
A MiG-31K carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile took off, triggering a country‑wide alert (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
Six Tu‑95MS and two Tu‑160 bombers are positioned for a massed missile strike, with up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles prepared (Source: @eRadarrua/94134).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity from the Black Sea heading south into Odesa and other oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63168).
A Russian UAV attacked a multi‑storey building in Galati, Romania, a NATO member (Source: @eRadarrua/94103).
Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Voronezh region was announced (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252).
Four controlled UAVs were observed over the sea near Chornomorsk (Source: @eRadarrua/94112).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia and a MiG‑31K with a Kinzhal missile triggered nationwide alerts. Ukraine reported extensive UAV incursions across multiple fronts, including a Russian drone strike on a Romanian building. Russia is massing strategic bombers and cruise missiles for a large‑scale strike.

points: Ukrainian sources reported a UAV from the Black Sea moving south into Odesa region, indicating continued maritime drone threats (Source: @kpszsu/63168).
Four controlled UAVs were observed over the sea near Chornomorsk, suggesting reconnaissance or strike preparation (Source: @eRadarrua/94112).
Multiple UAV groups were noted moving from various oblasts toward strategic targets, including a group from Chernihiv heading to Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63130).
Ukrainian officials highlighted the need for increased defense funding and procurement of Gripen fighters, though delivery expected 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
A MiG‑31K equipped with an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile took off, prompting a nationwide air alarm (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
Six Tu‑95MS bombers relocated to Engels airfield and two Tu‑160 bombers positioned at Ukrainianka, preparing up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles for a massed attack (Source: @eRadarrua/94134).
Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region was announced (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252).
UAVs from the Black Sea were reported moving south into Odesa and towards the southern direction of Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/63170).
Guided bomb attacks by Russian tactical aviation targeted Zaporizhzhia, a key eastern industrial region (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
A new reactive UAV from Bryansk was observed heading to Horodnia in Chernihiv region, indicating cross‑border drone activity (Source: @eRadarrua/94082).
Ukrainian reports noted a UAV group from the southeast of Dnipropetrovsk moving toward Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/63172).
UAVs were observed over northern Poltava heading toward Pyriatyn (Source: @kpszsu/63184).
A UAV group from Kursk region moved toward Sumy, indicating northern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/63158).
UAV activity from Chernihiv heading toward Kyiv region, course toward Chernobyl (Source: @kpszsu/63130).
A UAV from the Black Sea moved south into Odesa, showing northern‑to‑southern transit routes (Source: @kpszsu/63168).
UAVs from the Black Sea moved south into Odesa and towards the southern direction of Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/63170).
A new reactive UAV from Bryansk was reported heading to Horodnia and other southern oblasts including Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/94082).
Four UAVs were seen over the sea near Chornomorsk, a southern coastal city (Source: @eRadarrua/94112).
A Russian UAV attacked a multi‑storey building in Galati, Romania, indicating southern operational reach (Source: @eRadarrua/94103).
Russian MiG‑31K carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile took off, prompting a nationwide alert (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
Guided bomb sorties by Russian tactical aviation targeted Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Six Tu‑95MS and two Tu‑160 bombers are positioned for a massed strike with up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/94134).
Ukrainian reports of numerous UAV incursions across multiple fronts, including Black Sea, Chernihiv, and southern oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63168).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, indicating intensified air strikes on eastern targets. (Source: @kpszsu/63181)
A MiG‑31K equipped with an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile took off, triggering a nationwide air alert across Ukraine. (Source: @eRadarrua/94100)
Six Tu‑95MS and two Tu‑160 bombers are positioned for a massed missile strike, with up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles prepared for launch. (Source: @eRadarrua/94134)
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity from the Black Sea moving south into Odesa and Mykolaiv regions, showing maritime drone threats. (Source: @kpszsu/63168)
Four controlled UAVs were observed over the sea near Chornomorsk, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions in the southern coastal area. (Source: @eRadarrua/94112)
A Russian UAV attacked a multi‑storey building in Galati, Romania, a NATO member, marking a cross‑border escalation. (Source: @eRadarrua/94103)
Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region was announced, raising concerns of long‑range strike capability. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air operations have escalated with guided bomb strikes, strategic bomber deployments, and the use of a Kinzhal‑capable MiG‑31K, increasing pressure on Ukrainian air defenses. UAV incursions across multiple fronts, including maritime routes, complicate detection and interception efforts. The announced Iskander‑M threat adds a long‑range ballistic dimension, potentially targeting rear areas and critical infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air defense alerts nationwide and monitoring UAV activity across northern, eastern, and southern fronts. NATO member Romania has lodged diplomatic protests over the drone strike, indicating potential regional escalation. International partners continue to discuss increased defense aid, including future Gripen deliveries, though immediate operational impact remains limited.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued UAV traffic from the Black Sea suggests a supply or reconnaissance corridor that may be exploited for future strikes. The massing of strategic bombers and cruise missiles points to a planned large‑scale offensive, requiring heightened readiness of missile defense assets. Monitoring of Iskander‑M launch preparations in Voronezh is critical for early warning of potential long‑range attacks.
